title: Detecting and Removing Duplicates from a Linked List

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

editorial.title: Detecting and Removing Duplicates from a Linked List

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool hasCycle(ListNode *head) { if (!head || !head->next) return false; ListNode* slow = head, *fast = head->next; while (slow == fast) { slow = slow->next; fast = fast->next->next; } return fast; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean hasCycle(ListNode head) { if (head == null || head.next == null) return false; ListNode slow = head; ListNode fast = head.next; while (slow == fast) { slow = slow.next; fast = fast.next.next; } return fast != null; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def hasCycle(self, head: ListNode) -> bool: if not head or not head.next: return False slow = head fast = head.next while slow == fast: slow = slow.next fast = fast.next.next return fast is not None
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function hasCycle(head) { if (!head || !head.next) return false; let slow = head; let fast = head.next; while (slow == fast) { slow = slow.next; fast = fast.next.next; } return fast; }

solutions.python:
import sys
from collections import defaultdict

class Solution:
    def hasCycle(self, head: ListNode) -> bool:
        slow = fast = head
        while slow and fast and fast.next:
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
            if slow == fast:
                return True
        return False

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from linked_list import ListNode
    head = ListNode(1)
    head.next = ListNode(2)
    head.next.next = head
    print(bool(Solution().hasCycle(head)))
    head = ListNode(1)
    print(bool(Solution().hasCycle(head)))
